county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Missouri county formed 1845 from Daviess. County seat: Bethany.
county_metadata.research_tips: Recorder of Deeds in Bethany holds land records from 1845. Harrison County Historical Society in Bethany has genealogical files. North-central Missouri county near the Iowa border. Grand River basin settlement. Strong Midwestern farming heritage with some Southern settlement from Kentucky. FamilySearch microfilm covers deed books and probate records. Missouri State Archives holds circuit court records. BLM GLO records document original land entries. Bethany served as a regional market town.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO original land patents; county Recorder of Deeds records from 1845

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Missouri county formed 1834 from Lillard (renamed Lafayette). County seat: Clinton.
county_metadata.research_tips: Recorder of Deeds in Clinton holds records with some gaps from Civil War guerrilla damage in the 1860s. Henry County (originally Rives County, renamed 1841) was on the border of Order No. 11 territory. For pre-war records, check Lafayette County (parent) and Missouri State Archives. Henry County Historical Society in Clinton has post-war genealogical files. Clinton had significant German immigrant community. Golden Valley area settlement history. FamilySearch microfilm covers available records. Missouri State Archives holds some surviving early circuit court records.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO original land patents; county Recorder of Deeds records from 1834

county_metadata.record_losses: 1852 courthouse fire; 1881 fire; most early records destroyed
county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Missouri county formed 1845 from Benton, Polk. County seat: Hermitage. Burned county: 1852 courthouse fire; 1881 fire; most early records destroyed
county_metadata.research_tips: Recorder of Deeds in Hermitage holds records restarting after the 1852 and 1881 fires that destroyed most early records. For pre-fire research, check Benton and Polk counties (parents), Missouri State Archives, and BLM GLO records. Hickory County is one of Missouri's smallest and most rural counties. Pomme de Terre Lake and Truman Lake now cover former settlement areas — check Corps of Engineers relocation records. Ozark heritage with Southern settlement. FamilySearch microfilm covers post-fire records. Hickory County Historical Society has limited but valuable post-fire collections.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO original land patents; county Recorder of Deeds records from 1845

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Missouri county formed 1841 from Platte Purchase. County seat: Oregon. Part of the Platte Purchase (1836) — last major land acquisition from Native Americans in Missouri.
county_metadata.research_tips: Recorder of Deeds in Oregon holds land records from 1841. Holt County was part of the Platte Purchase (1836) — pre-1836 records are federal Indian agency files at NARA-KC. Northwestern Missouri county on the Missouri River. Holt County Historical Society has genealogical files. Big Lake State Park area — Missouri River flooding has repeatedly affected records and settlements. Strong Midwestern farming community. FamilySearch microfilm covers deed books and probate. BLM GLO records document original land entries.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO original land patents; county Recorder of Deeds records from 1841

county_metadata.parent_county: Cooper (originally St. Louis and St. Charles districts)
county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Missouri county formed 1816 from Cooper (originally St. Louis and St. Charles districts). County seat: Fayette. Historic Boonslick region — earliest area of American settlement west of St. Louis. Part of Missouri's 'Little Dixie' — strong Southern heritage and slaveholding region.
county_metadata.research_tips: Recorder of Deeds in Fayette holds land records from 1816. Howard County is one of the oldest counties in Missouri — the 'Mother of Counties' from which many others were carved. The Boonslick region was the earliest area of significant American settlement west of St. Louis. Howard County Historical Society has genealogical files. Central Methodist University in Fayette has local history collections. Significant enslaved persons records — Howard County was one of Missouri's leading slaveholding counties. 'Boonslick country' attracted Virginia and Kentucky settlers. FamilySearch microfilm is extensive. Arrow Rock State Historic Site preserves early settlement history.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO original land patents; county Recorder of Deeds records from 1816

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Missouri county formed 1857 from Oregon. County seat: West Plains. Ozark Highlands region with rugged terrain and Southern Appalachian settlement.
county_metadata.research_tips: Recorder of Deeds in West Plains holds records restarting after Civil War destruction. Howell County suffered significant courthouse record losses during guerrilla warfare. For pre-war records, check Oregon County (parent), Missouri State Archives, and BLM GLO land patents. West Plains was a contested area between Union and Confederate forces. Howell County Historical Society in West Plains has post-war genealogical files. Strong Southern Appalachian settlement heritage. South-central Ozarks terrain limited early settlement. FamilySearch microfilm covers post-war records.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO original land patents; county Recorder of Deeds records from 1857

county_metadata.parent_county: Madison, Reynolds, St. Francois, Washington, Wayne
county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Missouri county formed 1857 from Madison, Reynolds, St. Francois, Washington, Wayne. County seat: Ironton. Ozark Highlands region with rugged terrain and Southern Appalachian settlement. Missouri Lead Mining District — lead mining from French colonial era to present.
county_metadata.research_tips: Recorder of Deeds in Ironton holds land records from 1857. Iron County was named for the iron ore deposits in the area — Pilot Knob and Ironton were major iron production centers. Battle of Pilot Knob (1864) was a significant Civil War engagement — courthouse survived but area was devastated. Iron County Historical Society in Ironton has genealogical files. Check Madison, Reynolds, St. Francois, Washington, and Wayne counties for pre-1857 records. Strong German and Cornish miner immigration. Arcadia Valley settlement history. FamilySearch microfilm covers deed books and probate.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO original land patents; county Recorder of Deeds records from 1857

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Confederate and Union service records, loyalty oaths, provost marshal records, pension applications, and guerrilla warfare documentation for Iron area. Missouri's border state status means both Union and Confederate records exist.

source_profiles.repository: Missouri Mines State Historic Site / Missouri State Archives
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Mining claims, land patents, mining company property records, and associated community records for Iron County's lead mining operations. Missouri's Lead Belt was the nation's primary lead producer from colonial times.

county_metadata.record_losses: 1862 fire during Civil War; courthouse burned; significant record losses; Order No. 11 affected rural areas
county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Missouri county formed 1826 from Lafayette. County seat: Independence. Affected by Order No. 11 (1863) — forced depopulation of western Missouri border counties during Civil War. Burned county: 1862 fire during Civil War; courthouse burned; significant record losses; Order No. 11 affected rural areas
county_metadata.research_tips: Recorder of Deeds in Independence and Kansas City holds records restarting after the 1862 courthouse fire. Jackson County suffered severe Civil War damage — Order No. 11 affected rural areas and the courthouse was burned. Kansas City Public Library's Missouri Valley Special Collections is an outstanding genealogical resource. Independence was the starting point of the Oregon, California, and Santa Fe Trails — check NARA-KC for westward migration records. Harry S Truman Library in Independence has regional history collections. Jackson County Historical Society maintains extensive post-war genealogical files. Significant Mormon history — Independence was designated as 'Zion' by Joseph Smith. Kansas City had major German, Irish, Italian, and African American communities. City directories from 1860s.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO original land patents; county Recorder of Deeds records from 1826

county_metadata.record_losses: 1863 Battle of Carthage and guerrilla warfare; courthouse burned; pre-war records largely destroyed
county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Missouri county formed 1841 from Barry. County seat: Carthage. Burned county: 1863 Battle of Carthage and guerrilla warfare; courthouse burned; pre-war records largely destroyed
county_metadata.research_tips: Recorder of Deeds in Carthage holds records restarting after the 1863 Battle of Carthage and guerrilla warfare. The courthouse was burned and pre-war records largely destroyed. For pre-1863 records, check Barry County (parent), Missouri State Archives, and BLM GLO records. Jasper County Museum in Carthage has post-war genealogical collections. Joplin, Missouri's mining boom city, grew rapidly after the war — lead and zinc mining records at Missouri Mines State Historic Site. Significant immigrant communities (German, Irish, Italian miners). Bonnie and Clyde hideout history. FamilySearch microfilm covers post-war records. Powers Museum in Carthage has regional history collections.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO original land patents; county Recorder of Deeds records from 1841

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Missouri county formed 1818 from Ste. Genevieve, St. Louis. County seat: Hillsboro. French colonial heritage — area settled under French and Spanish governance before Louisiana Purchase (1803).
county_metadata.research_tips: Recorder of Deeds in Hillsboro holds land records from 1818. Jefferson County is south of St. Louis with significant mining heritage — lead and barite mining records. Jefferson County Historical Society in DeSoto has genealogical files. Sandy Creek Covered Bridge (1872) area settlement history. Strong French colonial heritage through Ste. Genevieve and St. Louis connections. German immigrant communities in the 1840s-1860s. FamilySearch microfilm covers deed books and probate. Missouri State Archives holds circuit court records. Herculaneum was an early lead smelting center — industrial records supplement genealogy.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Missouri State Archives microfilm and digitized records for Jefferson from colonial era

source_profiles.repository: Missouri State Archives, Jefferson City / Ste. Genevieve Archives
source_profiles.coverage_notes: French colonial court records, Spanish colonial land grants, parish registers, and administrative records for the Jefferson area. Louisiana Purchase (1803) transferred governance to the US.

source_profiles.repository: Local congregations / Concordia Historical Institute / Missouri State Archives
source_profiles.coverage_notes: German Evangelical, Lutheran, Catholic, and Reformed church records for Jefferson. Baptisms, marriages, burials, and confirmations. Many records in German language. Concordia Historical Institute (Lutheran Church-Missouri Synod) in St. Louis holds synod records.

source_profiles.repository: Missouri Mines State Historic Site / Missouri State Archives
